text: Research: Crypto Card Usage Up 2.7x Despite Market Cycles According to researcher Alex Obchakevich, analysis of 76 weeks of data across 16 crypto card providers shows transaction activity has grown 2.7x since January 2025, with no correlation to Bitcoin's price. Median top-ups remain in the $90–$135 range, while deposit behavior has become more evenly distributed, suggesting crypto cards are increasingly being used as everyday payment infrastructure rather than speculative tools. — link

text: The Millennial Midlife Crisis is Going to be a Barbell - Millennials are hitting the dip with no Corvette, no house, and, for the first time in consumer history, no appetite (see: peptides) A behavioral "barbell," split between loud end focused on public optimization (like extreme fitness and performance metrics) and a quiet end focused on complete retreat (such as private group chats, analog luxury, and synthetic AI companionship) Driven by structural economic limitations, career anxieties from AI, and the widespread use of appetite-suppressing GLP-1 peptides, this shift is hollowing out traditional impulse-driven consumer markets and giving rise to a new era of scarcity-based, community-driven brands
author: admin#1742094092

text: The EU's MiCA transition period will end on July 1, after which crypto exchanges, brokers and wallet service providers without a MiCA license will no longer be able to serve EU users. As of May 2026, only 194 crypto firms in the EU had obtained a license, compared with more than 3,000 registered crypto firms in 2024. Around 75% of existing firms are expected to lose operating eligibility. Unlicensed platforms will need to stop new deposits and guide users to withdraw assets or transfer them to licensed firms. Regulators in some countries may also take measures such as website blocking and public warning lists. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: A frontier without an ecosystem is not stable AI is enabling a new cognitive loop between humans and systems, shifting enterprises from using digital tools to enhance human capital toward compounding human judgment with proprietary AI "token capital" that encodes organizational expertise Companies must build agentic systems and private learning loops using internal workflows and reinforcement learning on real traces to create compounding IP, allowing model swaps without losing firm-specific knowledge

text: Macro Memo: Spin Cycle Despite short-term volatility and recent concerns over inflation and potential rate hikes under the new Fed, the broader outlook remains medium-term bullish on US equities and the economy due to stable core inflation, a non-tight labor market, and a likely easing of geopolitical energy pressures Expecting the AI-driven market rally to continue melting up into late summer, the recommended macro strategy is to fade the market's over-hawkish rate expectations
author: admin#1742094092
